target.title: feat(cli): add openclaw experimental for toggling experimental config flags
target.body:
> AI-assisted. Tested on pushed branch `115daf62fe`: focused Vitest coverage for config/CLI/chat/TUI passed, config schema check passed, the real `pnpm openclaw experimental --help` entrypoint resolved, the interactive CLI picker wrote a throwaway config with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif), a real TUI selector flow has visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif), and a rebuilt Docker gateway exercised `/experimental` over WebSocket with persisted config writes.

## Summary

- **Problem:** OpenClaw has experimental config flags spread through the config schema, but the user-facing toggle surface was drifting toward a CLI-only implementation. That would make `/experimental` unavailable from chat/TUI/channel command surfaces and would duplicate the flag registry outside the config contract.
- **Why it matters:** Experimental flags affect runtime behavior and should use one bounded, auditable config subset across CLI, chat, TUI, and channel plugins instead of hard-coded per-surface lists.
- **What changed:** Added a shared experimental-flag helper that discovers boolean config leaves under `experimental` from the base config schema and schema UI hints. The CLI `openclaw experimental`, chat `/experimental`, TUI command handling, and channel-command registry now use that same subset.
- **Config command policy:** Added `commands.experimental` so `/experimental` can be enabled or disabled alongside other configurable command surfaces.
- **What did not change:** No new experimental flags were introduced. No secrets, network calls, or provider runtime behavior changed.

## Scope Note

This PR started as a small CLI picker, but review feedback exposed a broader product/security boundary: if experimental config can be edited from one surface, the implementation should not create a CLI-only registry or drift from chat, TUI, and channel command handling. The current shape is intentionally larger because it moves `/experimental` behind a shared schema-derived subset and a configurable command gate instead of adding another one-off config editor.

The scope is still kept to the minimum shared surface needed for that boundary: no new experimental flags, no arbitrary config editor, no new network/runtime capability, no SDK export, and no separate per-surface experimental registry.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI
- [x] Config
- [x] Messaging / channel command surface
- [x] TUI
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: Discord suggestion from Json in #general
- Reviewer concern addressed: Peter Steinberger asked that this avoid config-schema drift, consider `/experimental` in chat/TUI, and keep config editing security-bounded.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A - new feature and follow-up design correction.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Focused local tests cover the shared flag registry, CLI apply behavior, chat command parsing/gating, channel command listing, and TUI command handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New top-level command: `openclaw experimental`
- New slash command: `/experimental`
- TUI command handling can list and update experimental flags through the same shared registry
- `commands.experimental` controls whether the experimental chat/channel command is enabled
- Experimental flags are discovered from the config schema instead of duplicated in each surface

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Config schema + UI hints
        |
        v
shared experimental flag subset
        |
        +-- openclaw experimental
        +-- /experimental in chat/channel command handling
        +-- TUI command backend/handlers
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No. The command writes only existing config paths that users can already edit through config tooling.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, `/experimental` is a new configurable command surface. It is gated by `commands.experimental` and is limited to schema-discovered boolean config leaves whose path contains an `experimental` segment.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- Security boundary: this does not make arbitrary config editable from chat/TUI. The editable subset is derived from the config schema and narrowed to experimental boolean leaves only.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu source checkout in Codex worktree
- Runtime: Node 22 / pnpm
- Branch SHA: `115daf62fe`
- Docker proof image: `openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest` (image `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`)
- Docker proof gateway: `pr76298-exp-proof-gateway`, `127.0.0.1:19982->18789`, health `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`

### Commands run locally

```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=180000 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/experimental-flags.test.ts src/config/experimental-config-file.test.ts src/commands/experimental.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-gating.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-parse.test.ts src/tui/commands.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
pnpm config:schema:check
pnpm openclaw experimental --help
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json pnpm openclaw experimental
jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json
OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway
docker run -d --name pr76298-exp-proof-gateway -p 127.0.0.1:19982:18789 ... openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest node dist/index.js gateway --bind lan --port 18789
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:19982/healthz
docker exec pr76298-exp-proof-gateway node --input-type=module -e '<WebSocket /experimental proof script>'
jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-docker-proof/config/openclaw.json
```

### Expected

The schema-derived experimental flag subset is stable, CLI/chat/TUI surfaces use it consistently, `/experimental` obeys command gating, the real CLI entrypoint resolves the new command, and a gateway client with the right operator scopes can list and toggle the bounded experimental config subset.

### Actual

Verified on pushed branch `115daf62fe`.

## Evidence

- [x] Focused Vitest run passed 5 shards / 128 tests after the final type-only commit.
- [x] `git diff --check` passed before the final commit; `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed for the final type-only commit.
- [x] `pnpm config:schema:check` passed (`[base-config-schema] ok`).
- [x] `pnpm openclaw experimental --help` executed through the real repo CLI entrypoint at SHA `c354c59`.
- [x] Interactive CLI picker executed on head `115daf6`, selected `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, printed the updated-config summary, and persisted the expected throwaway config. Slow VHS GIF: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif
- [x] TUI selector proof executed through the real `runTui` loop with an isolated fixture backend. The TUI opened `/experimental`, listed experimental flags, selected `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, printed `experimental enabled: agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, and logged `setExperimentalFlag`. VHS GIF: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif
- [x] Docker image rebuilt after the final commit and the proof container ran from image `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`.
- [x] Dockered gateway health returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`.
- [x] WebSocket `chat.send` proof through the gateway returned real `Config updated` replies for `/experimental off tools.experimental.planTool` and `/experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`.
- [x] Persisted config contains `tools.experimental.planTool: false`, `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: true`, and `meta.lastTouchedVersion: "2026.5.22"`.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: schema-derived flag listing/apply helpers, CLI command behavior, chat parse/gating/list/set behavior, TUI command handling, embedded trusted-local TUI config path, live CLI registration/help output, interactive CLI picker write behavior, real TUI selector behavior with an isolated backend, and Dockered gateway command behavior over WebSocket.
- Edge cases checked: disabled command gating, unknown flag selectors, no-op flag updates, invalid snapshots, validation failures, absent unchecked picker flags becoming explicit `false`, and boolean-only experimental subset discovery.
- What was not verified: native Telegram visible proof. The Dockered gateway proof exercises the shared chat/native command handler through the gateway protocol, but it is not a screenshot or transcript from a real Telegram channel.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: `openclaw experimental` and `/experimental` now use one schema-derived, bounded experimental config subset across CLI, chat/gateway, TUI, and channel-command handling. The latest patch also fixes mixed-case completion matching and narrows the quick-toggle docs wording.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu source checkout at `115daf62fe`; Docker gateway behavior proof captured at `c354c598f7` before the final type-only TUI backend interface commit; rebuilt Docker gateway image `openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest` with image id `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`; container `pr76298-exp-proof-gateway` bound to `127.0.0.1:19982->18789` with isolated config under `/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-docker-proof/config/openclaw.json`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran focused Vitest through `node scripts/test-projects.mjs`, `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`, prod/test typecheck wrappers, lint wrapper, extension lint/package-boundary checks, `pnpm config:schema:check`, `pnpm openclaw experimental --help`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json pnpm openclaw experimental`, rebuilt the Docker gateway with `OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway`, started a clean proof container, checked `/healthz`, then connected over WebSocket as `gateway-client` with `operator.write` and `operator.admin` scopes and sent `/experimental off tools.experimental.planTool`, `/experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, and `/experimental list`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Focused Vitest after final commit: passed 5 shards / 128 tests
Prod typecheck after final commit: core and extensions passed
Test typecheck after final commit: core and extensions passed
Lint after final commit: core/extensions/scripts passed
Extension boundary after final commit: channel lint, bundled lint, package compile, and package canary passed
Schema check: [base-config-schema] ok
CLI help: OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (c354c59), Usage: openclaw experimental [options]
Gateway health: {"ok":true,"status":"live"}

FINAL /experimental off tools.experimental.planTool: Config updated: tools.experimental.planTool=false

Interactive CLI picker slow VHS proof: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif
TUI selector VHS proof: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif
```

Persisted config excerpt after the gateway proof:

- Observed result after fix: The real CLI entrypoint resolves `openclaw experimental`; the interactive CLI picker writes the expected throwaway config with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif); the TUI selector opens and writes the expected experimental flag with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif); focused config/CLI/chat/TUI tests pass; the Dockered gateway accepts a scoped backend connection and the `/experimental` command path writes the expected bounded config values to disk.
- What was not tested: Native Telegram visible proof was requested from Mantis after this run and is pending. Broad remote Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` proof was attempted earlier, but the local Crabbox binary is missing, so CI on this pushed SHA is the current broad gate.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes

- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Chat/TUI config editing could accidentally grow into arbitrary config mutation.
  - **Mitigation:** The shared helper only exposes schema-discovered boolean leaves under an `experimental` path segment. `/experimental` is also gated by `commands.experimental`.
- **Risk:** Future experimental flags could drift from one surface.

target.title: Policy: add ingress channel conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds config-level ingress/channel conformance checks to the Policy plugin.

This PR now carries only the ingress layer. It was rebased on current main after the tool-posture work landed, and the sandbox-posture layer has been moved back to its own branch (`policy-sandbox-posture-conformance`) instead of being carried in this PR.

The new checks cover:

- `ingress.session.requireDmScope`
- `ingress.channels.allowDmPolicies`
- `ingress.channels.denyOpenGroups`
- `ingress.channels.requireMentionInGroups`

Policy observes configured `session.dmScope`, channel/account DM policy, channel/account group policy, and channel/group mention gates. It remains config-level conformance only: it does not inspect pairing stores, live membership, runtime sender authorization, or provider runtime defaults.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Policy conformance can now report nonconforming ingress/channel configuration for direct-message session scope, DM policy, open group ingress, and missing group mention gates.
Real environment tested: WSL checkout at /root/src/openclaw-policy-ingress-conformance on Ubuntu-24.04.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran focused Policy plugin doctor/CLI tests plus docs, formatting, and whitespace checks against the rebased ingress-only diff.
Evidence after fix: The focused tests exercise malformed ingress policy shape, nonconforming ingress findings, disabled channel/account filtering, nested mention overrides, inherited `channels.defaults.groupPolicy`, explicit posture requirements, and accepted inherited account posture.
Observed result after fix: 166 focused Vitest tests pass; docs checks, formatting checks, and whitespace checks pass.
What was not tested: Full repository test suite and live `openclaw policy check` command output.

target.title: fix(ui): show failed tool results as errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: failed tool results in chat could look like successful tool output with a checkmark.
- Solution: propagate explicit `isError` / `is_error` flags into tool cards and add bounded fallback detection for common error payloads.
- What changed: tool cards, standalone tool-result summaries, sidebar content, and expanded output labels now render failed tool results with error styling and copy.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no agent execution, gateway protocol, tool runtime, or NemoClaw code paths were changed.

## Motivation

- NVIDIA/NemoClaw#2621 showed that an OpenClaw UI surface could display a tool failure as normal output, which hid the real failure mode from users during triage.
- This should be fixed in OpenClaw because the underlying tool result already carries error information or an error payload; the UI was not consistently surfacing it.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related NVIDIA/NemoClaw#2621
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: chat tool cards and standalone tool-result summaries now show failed tool results as `Tool error` with error styling instead of `Tool output` / `View` with a success checkmark.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw worktree; Vite UI harness with system Chrome; focused Vitest UI tests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/types/chat-types.ts ui/src/styles/chat/tool-cards.css ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `codex review --base refs/remotes/tmp/openclaw-main-2621`
  - Temporary Vite proof page rendered `renderToolCardSidebar` in Chrome with one JSON error result and one successful result.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - Vitest: `Test Files 3 passed (3)`, `Tests 66 passed (66)`.
  - Format: `All matched files use the correct format.`
  - Whitespace: `git diff --check` exited 0.
  - Codex review: `I did not find a discrete regression in the modified surfaces.`
  - Browser harness: `cardCount=2`, `errorCards=1`, `errorActionText="View error"`, success card kept `View`.
- Observed result after fix: error payloads such as `{"error":"missing_brave_api_key"}` and exact `Tool not found` output render as failed tool UI, while normal outputs continue to render as successful output.
- What was not tested: full live OpenClaw agent plus Brave or NemoClaw sandbox end-to-end; earlier Linux latest-image repro was blocked by `no space left on device` while pulling the image.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing behavior treated top-level JSON error output as normal `Tool output` in grouped rendering and successful sidebar action copy.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the chat UI treated tool output text as successful unless the local rendering path explicitly knew otherwise, so common error payloads and standalone tool-result summaries were not consistently mapped to failed UI state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: focused UI tests did not assert that explicit error flags, JSON `error` payloads, `Tool not found`, and standalone collapsed tool results all render as errors.
- Contributing context (if known): OpenClaw has both explicit error flags (`isError`, `is_error`) and textual/JSON tool output surfaces, so the UI needs to respect both.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.node.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: explicit `isError` / `is_error` flags and fallback error outputs must render as `Tool error` with error styling in card, sidebar, expanded, and collapsed standalone summary paths.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests cover the UI normalization/rendering functions that decide the visible state, without requiring a live tool provider.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing tool card tests covered successful rendering; this PR extends them for failed rendering.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Failed tool results now show error labels, error badges, failed empty-output copy, and `View error` actions in the chat UI.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[tool result with error payload] -> [tool card] -> [Tool output / success checkmark]

After:
[tool result with error payload or isError flag] -> [tool card] -> [Tool error / error badge]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local development machine
- Runtime/container: local Node/pnpm UI workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): OpenClaw chat UI rendering
- Relevant config (redacted): no secrets required

### Steps

1. Render tool cards with a JSON error payload, an explicit error flag, and a successful output.
2. Render grouped standalone tool-result messages in collapsed and expanded states.
3. Run focused chat rendering tests, formatting, whitespace check, and Codex review.

### Expected

- Failed tool results are visually and textually marked as errors.
- Successful tool results keep existing success copy and styling.

### Actual

- Failed results render as `Tool error`, include error styling/badges, and use `View error` in the sidebar card action.
- Successful results still render as `Tool output` / `View`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - JSON `error` payloads.
  - exact `Tool not found` output.
  - explicit `isError` and `is_error` flags.
  - `isError: false` combined with an error payload.
  - collapsed standalone tool-result summaries.
  - empty failed output sidebar copy.
  - successful outputs remain successful.
- Edge cases checked:
  - blank output is not treated as an error unless an explicit error flag is present.
  - non-JSON and JSON arrays are not treated as error payloads.
  - very large output skips fallback JSON parsing.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full live OpenClaw agent plus external web-search provider end-to-end.
  - Linux latest-image repro after the local Linux host ran out of disk space while pulling the latest image.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: fallback JSON error detection could overclassify a successful tool output that intentionally returns a top-level truthy `error` field as data.
  - Mitigation: explicit `isError` / `is_error` flags are preferred, fallback parsing is bounded to top-level JSON objects and common known error shapes, and tests cover success outputs remaining unchanged.

AI-assisted: yes, implemented with Codex and reviewed with `codex review`.

## Summary
- Mirror the accepted Codex app-server user prompt as soon as the turn has a stable id.
- Use the context session key for the early transcript mirror so WebChat/Control UI session attribution is not replaced by the sandbox session key.
- Reuse the existing `turnId:prompt` mirror identity and thread-scoped idempotency so the final transcript mirror dedupes the prompt.
- Add regression coverage for split `sessionKey` and `sandboxSessionKey` behavior.

Fixes #83528

## Proof
- Head: `562116b0ae8e73abaa6088273cd8a3cfeffa3012`.

target.title: fix(gateway): register chat abort controller before attachment prep
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84176. `chat.abort` sent right after `chat.send` returned `aborted:false` because the abort controller was only registered after the awaited attachment preparation, leaving a window where the run had no entry in `chatAbortControllers`.

The fix mirrors the agent RPC early-registration pattern (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts:1746`):

- Register the abort controller before attachment prep so a concurrent `chat.abort` immediately observes the active run.
- Clean it up via `activeRunAbort.cleanup()` if attachment prep throws, and persist a terminal `chat:<runId>` error dedupe entry so duplicate senders that already received `status:"in_flight"` (and later retries with the same idempotencyKey) see the cached failure instead of spawning a fresh run.
- After prep, check `activeRunAbort.controller.signal.aborted` — if `chat.abort` already fired, `abortChatRunById` has cleared the entry and broadcast `aborted`, so the handler skips dispatch and acknowledges with `status: "aborted"` instead of falsely emitting a `started` ack.
- Cache the aborted outcome under `chat:<runId>` so retries with the same `idempotencyKey` see the cached aborted result rather than spawning a new run (mirrors the existing `started`/`ok`/`error` dedupe writes).

The pre-existing `"duplicate attachment send wins parsing race"` test gets stricter expectations as a consequence: with early registration the first send always wins (`started`) and any duplicate before it finishes always sees `in_flight`, which matches idempotency semantics. Two new regression tests cover (a) abort-during-attachment-prep and (b) duplicate-in_flight then prep-failure.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: prior to this fix, `chat.send` only inserted the abort controller into `context.chatAbortControllers` after `await gateway.chat_send.prepare_attachments`. A `chat.abort` arriving while prep was still pending called `chatAbortControllers.get(runId)`, found nothing, and returned `{ ok: true, aborted: false, runIds: [] }` even though the chat run was still active.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Ubuntu 22.04, Node v24.15.0, with the production `chatHandlers["chat.send"]` and `chatHandlers["chat.abort"]` driven directly through `node --import tsx` against a real `Map` for `chatAbortControllers`/`dedupe`, real `AbortController`, and a deferred model-catalog mock that suspends chat.send mid attachment prep so chat.abort can race with it.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx /tmp/probe-handler-84176.mjs` — the script imports the live `chatHandlers` map, fires `chat.send` with an image attachment, waits until the handler has registered the abort controller (early registration is the fix), fires `chat.abort` for the same runId, then releases the catalog so chat.send finishes and prints the resulting handler responses plus the cached dedupe entry.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "registeredBeforeAbort": true,
  "registeredAfterAbort": false,
  "abortResponse": {
    "ok": true,
    "payload": {
      "ok": true,
      "aborted": true,
      "runIds": [
        "probe-run-84176"
      ]
    }
  },
  "sendResponse": {
    "ok": true,
    "payload": {
      "runId": "probe-run-84176",
      "status": "aborted",
      "stopReason": "rpc"
    }
  },
  "cachedChatRunDedupe": {
    "ok": true,
    "payload": {
      "runId": "probe-run-84176",
      "status": "aborted",
      "stopReason": "rpc"
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix, driven through the real handlers:

- `chat.send` registered the abort controller before attachment prep finished, so `chat.abort` for the same runId returned `{ ok: true, aborted: true, runIds: [<runId>] }` instead of the buggy `aborted: false`.
- After abort, `chatAbortControllers` no longer held the entry (cleared by `abortChatRunById`).
- The suspended `chat.send`, once the catalog resolved and prep finished, detected `signal.aborted` and responded with `status: "aborted", stopReason: "rpc"` instead of falsely emitting `started` and dispatching the run.
- The aborted outcome was cached under `chat:<runId>` so a retry with the same `idempotencyKey` returns the cached aborted ack instead of starting a new run.

What was not tested: no full end-to-end gateway WS roundtrip against a live model provider; the new regression tests in `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts` exercise the same handler entry points with a real `Map` for `chatAbortControllers`/`dedupe` and a deferred catalog mock.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/` — 223 files / 2585 tests passed, including the two new regression tests (`chat.abort during attachment prep finds the active run (regression for #84176)` and `chat.send attachment-prep failure caches the error so a duplicate that already saw in_flight does not retry into a fresh run`).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.abort-authorization.test.ts` — the three test files ClawSweeper named in its review of #84176 all pass.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts` — clean.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs` — clean.
- Auto Review (extra-high recall, 5 angles + verifier + sweep): one HIGH finding fixed (aborted-path missing `chat:<runId>` dedupe write); one LOW finding fixed (test used `.find()` without count assertion).
- ClawSweeper P2 follow-up (`chat.ts:2481`): the second commit adds the prep-failure dedupe write and its regression test.

target.title: fix: preflight malformed OpenShell exec commands
target.body:
﻿Fixes #72373.

## Summary
- Add a shared SSH/OpenShell exec command preflight before remote `/bin/sh -c` execution.
- Reject unresolved generated placeholders like `<workflow-id>` and common malformed shell input such as unclosed quotes, trailing escapes, unterminated command substitutions, and unterminated here-docs.
- Preserve legitimate shell syntax including redirection, compact redirection, here-doc bodies, process substitution, arithmetic expansion, quoted placeholder-looking text, and comments.
- Build validated remote exec commands before opening SSH/OpenShell sessions so malformed input fails before session setup.
- Preserve the existing public `buildExecRemoteCommand` quote-only contract, and route SSH/OpenShell runtime paths through the explicitly named `buildValidatedExecRemoteCommand` helper.

This follows ClawSweeper's suggested fix shape: keep the existing loop-detection behavior and add the remaining focused validator at the shared SSH/OpenShell command boundary.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: malformed generated exec commands such as `workflow run <workflow-id> --ref main` now fail before SSH/OpenShell session or runtime setup.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw PR head `07bfc06675` on my Windows desktop, Docker Desktop `29.4.1`, a disposable Debian `bookworm-slim` container running a real `sshd`, Windows OpenSSH client, and OpenClaw's real `createSshSandboxBackend` / `buildExecSpec` path. This exercises the shared SSH exec boundary used by OpenShell; no VPS or live OpenShell account was used for this proof.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started a throwaway Docker SSH server, created a real SSH sandbox backend pointed at it, ran one valid backend exec to prove the remote path worked, then created a fresh backend and attempted `workflow run <workflow-id> --ref main`.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```text
REAL_SSH_VALID_EXIT=0
REAL_SSH_VALID_STDOUT="/tmp/openclaw-72373-proof/openclaw-ssh-pr85874-proof-valid-d6495223/workspace\nseed-from-host"
MALFORMED_COMMAND_ERROR="Malformed SSH/OpenShell exec command: unresolved placeholder token <workflow-id>."
BAD_RUNTIME_ID=openclaw-ssh-pr85874-proof-bad-1e0d9052
BAD_RUNTIME_CHECK="RUNTIME_NOT_CREATED"
REMOTE_CLEANUP="REMOTE_CLEANED"
LOCAL_PROOF_CLEANUP=done
REMOVED_CONTAINER=oc72373-proof-1779595741
```

- Observed result after fix: the valid real SSH backend command executed and read the synced workspace seed file. The malformed generated command failed with the new preflight error, and the fresh bad-command runtime directory was not created on the remote SSH host.
- What was not tested: a live NVIDIA OpenShell CLI/account/gateway run. The proof uses the real SSH backend because the fixed validator and fail-before-session behavior are in the shared SSH/OpenShell exec boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current main passed raw `params.command` into the shared `/bin/sh -c` command builder without this preflight, and OpenShell built the command only after sandbox/session setup.

## Proof
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts -t "keeps the public exec command builder quote-only|rejects malformed generated exec commands|allows shell features and quoted placeholder-looking text|uses the shared SSH exec command preflight|rejects malformed exec commands before opening" --reporter verbose` -> passed, 3 files / 26 tests passed, 30 skipped
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/sandbox/ssh.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh-backend.ts src/agents/sandbox.ts src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.ts extensions/openshell/src/backend.ts extensions/openshell/src/cli.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts` -> passed
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` -> passed
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed
- `pnpm tsgo:test` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

## Root Cause
- Root cause: generated placeholder-like shell text could reach the SSH/OpenShell runtime command boundary without validation.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the runtime path only quoted the final shell argv; it did not reject malformed generated commands before runtime/session setup.
- Contributing context: the issue's loop-abort half is already handled on main, so this PR intentionally keeps the fix focused on the remaining SSH/OpenShell command-preflight gap.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this: unit tests plus backend-boundary regression tests.
- Target test or file: `src/agents/sandbox/ssh.test.ts` and `extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: malformed placeholder commands, unclosed quotes, trailing escapes, and unterminated shell constructs fail before SSH/OpenShell session setup, while valid shell syntax remains allowed.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives at the shared command construction boundary used by both SSH and OpenShell exec paths. The PR also locks the public `buildExecRemoteCommand` helper as quote-only for compatibility.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Malformed generated SSH/OpenShell exec commands now fail fast with a clear `Malformed SSH/OpenShell exec command` error instead of being handed to the remote shell. The existing public `buildExecRemoteCommand` helper remains quote-only for plugin compatibility.

target.title: fix(embedded-runner): keep active compaction retries from looking stuck
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: long-running embedded runs could be misclassified as stuck while compaction retry work was still actively in flight.
- Why it matters: stuck-session recovery could treat active work as invalid or stalled work, which risks incorrect cleanup behavior for long-running tasks.
- What changed: diagnostic session progress is refreshed before long embedded prompt work and while compaction retries remain active, and regression tests were added to lock in the retry heartbeat path and observe-only stuck recovery behavior.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not change stuck-session recovery policy, abort thresholds, cleanup strategy outside this path, or any integration/channel-specific behavior.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Active compaction retry waits should keep refreshing diagnostic session activity so long-running embedded work is preserved instead of being misclassified as stuck when active abort recovery remains disabled.
Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw setup on device 25 (Ubuntu), source-built checkout at /home/ghost/src/openclaw-ghost, branch fix/stuck-task-heartbeat-upstream.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: On device 25, in /home/ghost/src/openclaw-ghost, run pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts, then inspect the live terminal output from that run and confirm both regression tests pass after the fix.
Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured from the real device 25 run after the fix.
```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.logging.config.ts
PASS logging src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts (11 tests)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
PASS agents src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts (7 tests)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 24.86s
```
Observed result after fix: The real device 25 run completed successfully; the logging regression test passed with 11 tests, the aggregate-timeout regression test passed with 7 tests, and the Vitest shard run finished in 24.86 seconds.
What was not tested: I did not run a full end-to-end synthetic harness covering every stuck-session recovery mode.

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Refs #77969
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TTS can turn emoji and decorative symbols into noisy spoken names. Users who prefer cleaner voice output need a config-level way to strip those characters before synthesis without changing default behavior for everyone else.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `tts-skip-v3`, commit `06545b841d08`, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (`extensions/microsoft` -> `node-edge-tts` -> `speech.platform.bing.com`). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- After-fix evidence:
  ```text
  $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
  # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
  TS
  {
    "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
    "commit": "06545b841d08",
    "provider": "microsoft",
    "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
    "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
    "observed": [
      {
        "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
        "bytes": 39456,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      },
      {
        "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
        "bytes": 36288,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      }
    ],
    "emptyAfterStrip": {
      "success": false,
      "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
      "provider": null
    },
    "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
  }

  $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
  ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       32 passed (32)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       14 passed (14)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing `✓`, `©`, and `🫠`; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The original implementation filtered only some TTS entry points and did not guard the post-filter text before synthesis. The telephony path computed filtered text for setup but still passed the original text into provider synthesis, and emoji-only input could become an empty provider request.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
  - stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
  - telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can enable `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` to strip emoji/symbol characters before speech synthesis. Unset or `false` preserves existing behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
text -> effective TTS config -> optional emoji/symbol strip -> empty guard -> provider synthesis
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: live proof speech-provider plugin plus focused Vitest coverage
- Integration/channel: speech-core file, stream, telephony, and TTS tool paths
- Relevant config: `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols=true|false`

### Steps

1. Run the live proof script shown above.
2. Run focused speech-core tests.
3. Run focused TTS tool tests.
4. Run test and production type gates.

### Expected

Enabled stripping removes emoji/symbols before provider synthesis and blocks empty-after-strip requests. Disabled mode preserves original text.

### Actual

The proof and tests match the expected behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Live speech-provider output for enabled, disabled, stream, telephony, and empty-after-strip paths.
- Focused speech-core test output.
- Focused TTS tool test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: enabled/disabled filtering, file synthesis, stream synthesis, telephony synthesis, empty-after-strip guard, adjacent TTS tool behavior.
- Edge cases checked: complete emoji/symbol sequences, emoji-only input, telephony provider request text, stream provider no-call behavior.
- Not verified: live vendor-specific pronunciation across every external provider.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: optional new config `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols`; no change required when unset.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Unicode stripping removes characters a user wanted spoken.
- Mitigation: behavior is opt-in and disabled by default.
- Risk: filtering differs across TTS paths.
- Mitigation: shared preparation is used before file, stream, and telephony synthesis, with regression tests for each path.

## Summary

This PR moves the gateway services doctor pass onto the structured repair runner while keeping the legacy lint/read-only presentation intact.

- Splits gateway service detection from repair so doctor --fix can report dry-run changes, diffs, and service effects through the shared repair runner.

- Keeps platform notes positional and note-based so user-visible lint behavior remains the same.

target.title: feat(cli): add openclaw experimental for toggling experimental config flags
target.body:
> AI-assisted. Tested on pushed branch `115daf62fe`: focused Vitest coverage for config/CLI/chat/TUI passed, config schema check passed, the real `pnpm openclaw experimental --help` entrypoint resolved, the interactive CLI picker wrote a throwaway config with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif), a real TUI selector flow has visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif), and a rebuilt Docker gateway exercised `/experimental` over WebSocket with persisted config writes.

## Summary

- **Problem:** OpenClaw has experimental config flags spread through the config schema, but the user-facing toggle surface was drifting toward a CLI-only implementation. That would make `/experimental` unavailable from chat/TUI/channel command surfaces and would duplicate the flag registry outside the config contract.
- **Why it matters:** Experimental flags affect runtime behavior and should use one bounded, auditable config subset across CLI, chat, TUI, and channel plugins instead of hard-coded per-surface lists.
- **What changed:** Added a shared experimental-flag helper that discovers boolean config leaves under `experimental` from the base config schema and schema UI hints. The CLI `openclaw experimental`, chat `/experimental`, TUI command handling, and channel-command registry now use that same subset.
- **Config command policy:** Added `commands.experimental` so `/experimental` can be enabled or disabled alongside other configurable command surfaces.
- **What did not change:** No new experimental flags were introduced. No secrets, network calls, or provider runtime behavior changed.

## Scope Note

This PR started as a small CLI picker, but review feedback exposed a broader product/security boundary: if experimental config can be edited from one surface, the implementation should not create a CLI-only registry or drift from chat, TUI, and channel command handling. The current shape is intentionally larger because it moves `/experimental` behind a shared schema-derived subset and a configurable command gate instead of adding another one-off config editor.

The scope is still kept to the minimum shared surface needed for that boundary: no new experimental flags, no arbitrary config editor, no new network/runtime capability, no SDK export, and no separate per-surface experimental registry.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI
- [x] Config
- [x] Messaging / channel command surface
- [x] TUI
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: Discord suggestion from Json in #general
- Reviewer concern addressed: Peter Steinberger asked that this avoid config-schema drift, consider `/experimental` in chat/TUI, and keep config editing security-bounded.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A - new feature and follow-up design correction.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Focused local tests cover the shared flag registry, CLI apply behavior, chat command parsing/gating, channel command listing, and TUI command handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New top-level command: `openclaw experimental`
- New slash command: `/experimental`
- TUI command handling can list and update experimental flags through the same shared registry
- `commands.experimental` controls whether the experimental chat/channel command is enabled
- Experimental flags are discovered from the config schema instead of duplicated in each surface

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Config schema + UI hints
        |
        v
shared experimental flag subset
        |
        +-- openclaw experimental
        +-- /experimental in chat/channel command handling
        +-- TUI command backend/handlers
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No. The command writes only existing config paths that users can already edit through config tooling.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, `/experimental` is a new configurable command surface. It is gated by `commands.experimental` and is limited to schema-discovered boolean config leaves whose path contains an `experimental` segment.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- Security boundary: this does not make arbitrary config editable from chat/TUI. The editable subset is derived from the config schema and narrowed to experimental boolean leaves only.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu source checkout in Codex worktree
- Runtime: Node 22 / pnpm
- Branch SHA: `115daf62fe`
- Docker proof image: `openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest` (image `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`)
- Docker proof gateway: `pr76298-exp-proof-gateway`, `127.0.0.1:19982->18789`, health `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`

### Commands run locally

```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=180000 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/experimental-flags.test.ts src/config/experimental-config-file.test.ts src/commands/experimental.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-gating.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-parse.test.ts src/tui/commands.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
pnpm config:schema:check
pnpm openclaw experimental --help
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json pnpm openclaw experimental
jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json
OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway
docker run -d --name pr76298-exp-proof-gateway -p 127.0.0.1:19982:18789 ... openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest node dist/index.js gateway --bind lan --port 18789
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:19982/healthz
docker exec pr76298-exp-proof-gateway node --input-type=module -e '<WebSocket /experimental proof script>'
jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-docker-proof/config/openclaw.json
```

### Expected

The schema-derived experimental flag subset is stable, CLI/chat/TUI surfaces use it consistently, `/experimental` obeys command gating, the real CLI entrypoint resolves the new command, and a gateway client with the right operator scopes can list and toggle the bounded experimental config subset.

### Actual

Verified on pushed branch `115daf62fe`.

## Evidence

- [x] Focused Vitest run passed 5 shards / 128 tests after the final type-only commit.
- [x] `git diff --check` passed before the final commit; `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed for the final type-only commit.

- [x] TUI selector proof executed through the real `runTui` loop with an isolated fixture backend. The TUI opened `/experimental`, listed experimental flags, selected `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, printed `experimental enabled: agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, and logged `setExperimentalFlag`. VHS GIF: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif
- [x] Docker image rebuilt after the final commit and the proof container ran from image `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`.

- [x] Persisted config contains `tools.experimental.planTool: false`, `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: true`, and `meta.lastTouchedVersion: "2026.5.22"`.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: schema-derived flag listing/apply helpers, CLI command behavior, chat parse/gating/list/set behavior, TUI command handling, embedded trusted-local TUI config path, live CLI registration/help output, interactive CLI picker write behavior, real TUI selector behavior with an isolated backend, and Dockered gateway command behavior over WebSocket.
- Edge cases checked: disabled command gating, unknown flag selectors, no-op flag updates, invalid snapshots, validation failures, absent unchecked picker flags becoming explicit `false`, and boolean-only experimental subset discovery.
- What was not verified: native Telegram visible proof. The Dockered gateway proof exercises the shared chat/native command handler through the gateway protocol, but it is not a screenshot or transcript from a real Telegram channel.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: `openclaw experimental` and `/experimental` now use one schema-derived, bounded experimental config subset across CLI, chat/gateway, TUI, and channel-command handling. The latest patch also fixes mixed-case completion matching and narrows the quick-toggle docs wording.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu source checkout at `115daf62fe`; Docker gateway behavior proof captured at `c354c598f7` before the final type-only TUI backend interface commit; rebuilt Docker gateway image `openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest` with image id `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`; container `pr76298-exp-proof-gateway` bound to `127.0.0.1:19982->18789` with isolated config under `/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-docker-proof/config/openclaw.json`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran focused Vitest through `node scripts/test-projects.mjs`, `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`, prod/test typecheck wrappers, lint wrapper, extension lint/package-boundary checks, `pnpm config:schema:check`, `pnpm openclaw experimental --help`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json pnpm openclaw experimental`, rebuilt the Docker gateway with `OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway`, started a clean proof container, checked `/healthz`, then connected over WebSocket as `gateway-client` with `operator.write` and `operator.admin` scopes and sent `/experimental off tools.experimental.planTool`, `/experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, and `/experimental list`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Focused Vitest after final commit: passed 5 shards / 128 tests
Prod typecheck after final commit: core and extensions passed
Test typecheck after final commit: core and extensions passed
Lint after final commit: core/extensions/scripts passed

CLI help: OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (c354c59), Usage: openclaw experimental [options]

TUI selector VHS proof: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif
```

- Observed result after fix: The real CLI entrypoint resolves `openclaw experimental`; the interactive CLI picker writes the expected throwaway config with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif); the TUI selector opens and writes the expected experimental flag with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif); focused config/CLI/chat/TUI tests pass; the Dockered gateway accepts a scoped backend connection and the `/experimental` command path writes the expected bounded config values to disk.

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494 HEAD_SHORT=c4a2be36ad node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram account media pre-download proof (c4a2be36ad, c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

target.title: feat(gateway): wire read coding tool into HTTP /tools/invoke (narrow)
target.body:
## Summary

Narrow first landing of the broader direct-invoke umbrella ([#37131](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/37131)). Wires a single coding primitive — **`read`** — into the HTTP `/tools/invoke` endpoint so deterministic automation flows (CI preflight, lint, browser capture) can read files without a full LLM round-trip.

This PR intentionally does **not** include mutating coding tools (`write`/`edit`/`exec`/`process`). They are out of scope here and remain unreachable on the `/tools/invoke` surface. Once read-only is accepted, a follow-up PR may opt mutating tools in via `gateway.tools.allow` + deny-list expansion (see [#63919](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63919) for that broader scope).

Sibling work: [#74804](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/74804) (merged by @BunsDev, 2026-05-01) added the SDK RPC `tools.invoke` method via the shared `tool-resolution.ts` resolver. **Important boundary clarification**: the `surface === "http"` gate this PR uses is set by `tools-invoke-shared.ts` for BOTH HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke` (they share the resolver). So allowlisting `read` exposes it consistently on both direct-invoke surfaces — same shared-logic pattern #74804 established for the broader infrastructure. MCP loopback (`surface === "loopback"`) is unaffected.

## Linked Issue

- Refs #37131 (Invoke tools directly without LLM round-trip)
- Sibling: #74804 (RPC `tools.invoke` — already merged)
- Follow-up: #63919 (broader scope with mutating tool opt-in — held for separate maintainer decision)
- [x] This PR adds new functionality (narrow capability)

## Implementation

1. **`src/agents/pi-tools.ts`** — add `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw()` (thin wrapper around the existing `createOpenClawCodingTools` factory) with a new `skipBeforeToolCallHook` option. Passing wrapped tools to the HTTP path would double-fire the `before_tool_call` hook (which `handleToolsInvokeHttpRequest` already runs itself before dispatch) and leak adjusted-params state.

2. **`src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`** — at `surface === "http"` only, call the raw factory and filter to `read`. The MCP loopback path (which uses the same resolver but does not apply `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`) is not affected. Gateway tools take precedence on name collision.

3. **`src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts`** — two new regression tests:
   - `read` is reachable when `gateway.tools.allow` includes it (HTTP 200, observed input matches request).
   - Mutating coding tools (write/edit/exec/process) remain unreachable on this surface (HTTP 404 even with explicit allow).

## Security

Narrow opt-in expansion: `read` is added to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`, so the operator must **explicitly opt in** via `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]` before it's reachable over HTTP. Same contract as the other coding primitives (exec, apply_patch, fs_write, etc.). No tool reaches the HTTP surface without operator acceptance.

No new dependency, workflow, permission, secret, or supply-chain surface is added by this diff.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `read` was not registered for the HTTP `/tools/invoke` surface in upstream — `createOpenClawTools()` (the existing gateway tool factory) doesn't include it. So `POST /tools/invoke {"tool":"read"}` returned `404 not_found` even when `gateway.tools.allow` named it. This PR wires it through.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `openclaw-85664-proof-95a8276f` built fresh from current PR head `95a8276fb8` running `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` + `pnpm build:docker` inside a base `openclaw:main` linux/amd64 container with the PR source mounted at `/workspace`. Gateway started via `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/proof-config.json node /workspace/openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 19106 --auth none --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured` with `agents.list[].tools.allow=["read"]` and `gateway.tools.allow=["read"]`. Version banner inside the container confirms head SHA: `OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (95a8276)`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Built fresh `dist/` inside container `openclaw-85664-proof-95a8276f` from PR head via `node /workspace/openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 19106 --auth none --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured`, then ran 5 `curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:19106/tools/invoke` calls against the running gateway for `read`, `write`, `edit`, `exec`, `process` and captured the live HTTP response bodies + gateway log lines in the terminal capture below.
- **Evidence after fix**: Live `docker exec` + `curl` terminal capture against the rebuilt gateway, redacted runtime logs, copied live HTTP output:

  ```text
  $ docker exec openclaw-85664-proof-95a8276f node /workspace/openclaw.mjs --version
  OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (95a8276)

  $ docker exec openclaw-85664-proof-95a8276f curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:19106/tools/invoke \
      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d '{"tool":"read","args":{"path":"/tmp/proof-workspace/example.txt"},"sessionKey":"main"}'
  {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello from proof at 2026-05-23T18:20:23Z (95a8276)\n"}]}}

  $ docker exec openclaw-85664-proof-95a8276f curl -s -X POST .../tools/invoke -d '{"tool":"write",...}'

  $ docker exec openclaw-85664-proof-95a8276f curl -s -X POST .../tools/invoke -d '{"tool":"edit",...}'

  $ docker exec openclaw-85664-proof-95a8276f curl -s -X POST .../tools/invoke -d '{"tool":"exec",...}'

  $ docker exec openclaw-85664-proof-95a8276f curl -s -X POST .../tools/invoke -d '{"tool":"process",...}'

  # --- restart gateway WITHOUT gateway.tools.allow=["read"] to verify default-deny ---

  {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}

  $ docker exec openclaw-85664-proof-95a8276f tail -10 /tmp/gateway.log
  2026-05-23T18:20:39.032+00:00 [gateway] starting HTTP server...
  2026-05-23T18:20:45.126+00:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 18.3s)
  2026-05-23T18:20:48.277+00:00 [gateway] ready

  # --- SDK RPC `tools.invoke` surface (against rebuilt head `7987cdeb35`) ---
  # in-process Node driver imports `coreGatewayHandlers["tools.invoke"]` from
  # the compiled `dist/server-methods-*.js` and invokes the JSON-RPC handler
  # directly with `(success, result, error)` respond signature; same shared
  # resolver as HTTP, different transport.
  $ docker exec openclaw-85664-proof-7987cdeb node /tmp/rpc-proof.mjs
  [bundle] handler loaded: coreGatewayHandlers['tools.invoke']

  === A. RPC tools.invoke read WITH gateway.tools.allow=['read'] (opt-in) ===
    RPC respond payload: {"success":true,"result":{"ok":true,"toolName":"read","output":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello from RPC proof at 2026-05-23T19:06:36Z (7987cde)\n"}]},"source":"core"}}

  === B. RPC tools.invoke write (NOT materialized by this PR) ===
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: Live terminal captures above cover BOTH direct-invoke surfaces on rebuilt heads (HTTP on `95a8276fb8`, RPC on `7987cdeb35`):
  - HTTP `POST /tools/invoke`: (1) `read` returns HTTP 200 with actual file contents when operator opts in via `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]`; (2) `read` returns HTTP 404 `Tool not available: read` when operator has NOT opted in (the new `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` entry blocks even with agent-level `allow: ["read"]` policy); (3) `write`/`edit`/`exec`/`process` always return HTTP 404 `Tool not available: <name>`.
  - **SDK RPC `tools.invoke` (same shared resolver via `tools-invoke-shared.ts`)**: (A) `read` with opt-in returns `{"ok":true,"toolName":"read","output":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello from RPC proof at ... (7987cde)"}]},"source":"core"}` — actual file contents over RPC; (B) `write` returns `{"ok":false,"toolName":"write","error":{"code":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}`; (C) `exec` returns the same `not_found` shape. Confirms the exposure is consistent across both direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP + RPC), per the documented `surface === "http"` direct-invoke marker semantics.
  - Gateway version banner `OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (95a8276)` / `(7987cde)` confirms the gateway in each run was the PR's compiled bundle, not an older image.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end non-owner-scope rejection over the wire (out of scope for read-only since `read` is not owner-only); cron/automerge integration (no scheduled flows in this scope).
- [x] Test setup builds and validates with this PR applied: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` + `pnpm build:docker` + `pnpm test src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` → all green; live HTTP curl on rebuilt head verifies the read-only contract.

### Detailed evidence (terminal captures)

**Gateway startup banner (against rebuilt `95a8276fb8`):**

```text
$ docker exec openclaw-85664-proof-95a8276f node /workspace/openclaw.mjs --version

2026-05-23T08:52:21.532+00:00 [gateway] ready
```

**Live HTTP suite against the running gateway:**

```text
$ curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:19106/tools/invoke -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"tool":"read","args":{"path":"/tmp/proof-workspace/example.txt"},"sessionKey":"main"}'
{"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello from proof at 2026-05-23T18:20:23Z (95a8276)\n"}]}}
HTTP 200

$ curl -X POST .../tools/invoke -d '{"tool":"write","args":{...},"sessionKey":"main"}'
{"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
HTTP 404

$ curl -X POST .../tools/invoke -d '{"tool":"edit","args":{},"sessionKey":"main"}'
{"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: edit"}}
HTTP 404

$ curl -X POST .../tools/invoke -d '{"tool":"exec","args":{"command":"ls"},"sessionKey":"main"}'
{"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: exec"}}
HTTP 404

$ curl -X POST .../tools/invoke -d '{"tool":"process","args":{"action":"json","command":"ls"},"sessionKey":"main"}'
{"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: process"}}
HTTP 404
```

The contrast: `read` returns the actual file contents (200); every other coding tool returns 404 with `Tool not available: <name>` — confirming this PR's narrow scope (only `read` is materialized; mutating tools are unreachable on this surface).

### Addressed ClawSweeper findings (current head `8b3d1581bf`)

- **[P2] Pass a read-only construction plan before filtering** (`tool-resolution.ts:197`) — Fixed in `95a8276fb8`: pass an explicit `toolConstructionPlan` to `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw()` — `includeBaseCodingTools: true` plus all four other families false. Factory work is bounded to just the base coding-tool family (read/write/edit/apply_patch), then filtered to `read`. No plugin/channel materialization.
- **[P2] Update the cron regression pi-tools mock** — Fixed in `532ec9b9fb`: the cron-regression test file's `vi.mock("../agents/pi-tools.js", ...)` was missing the new `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw` export, causing 9 cron-regression cases to 500. Added `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw: () => []` to the mock — cron-regression tests don't exercise the coding-tool path, so the resolver's filter is a no-op for those cases. 108/108 tests pass in the combined suite.
- **[P1] Keep the hook bypass out of the public SDK** (`pi-tools.ts:392`) — Fixed in `8b3d1581bf`: replaced the publicly-typed `skipBeforeToolCallHook` option with a module-private Symbol (`SKIP_BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_HOOK = Symbol("openclaw.pi-tools.skip-before-tool-call-hook")`). The Symbol is **not exported** — the only call site that sets it is `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw()` in the same file (the trusted internal HTTP-surface entry point). External SDK consumers cannot reach the hook-bypass code path:
  - The typed parameter shape of `createOpenClawCodingTools()` no longer mentions any hook-bypass option.
  - The check uses `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(options, SKIP_BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_HOOK)` with the module-private Symbol, so the property cannot be set by callers who lack a reference to the Symbol.
  - `Symbol()` (not `Symbol.for()`) produces a unique value — a caller reconstructing the description `"openclaw.pi-tools.skip-before-tool-call-hook"` would get a different Symbol that fails the `hasOwnProperty` check.

## Why narrow

Per ClawSweeper's "Narrow The First Landing" rank-up move on #63919: maintainer approval is easier for a smaller security-boundary expansion. `read` over authenticated HTTP is a much lower-risk decision than the full read+write+edit+exec+process surface in #63919.

target.title: Gateway: allow extension origins in browser allowlist
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` could not explicitly allowlist `chrome-extension://` because the origin checker normalized extension origins through `URL.origin`, which becomes `"null"` for that scheme. - Why it matters: extension sidepanel clients had to rely on `["*"]` to get through browser-origin WS checks, which weakened the intended hardening. - What changed: the origin matcher now keeps exact URL parsing/validation but falls back to exact `scheme://host` matching for non-standard origins, so `chrome-extension://` can be allowlisted exactly; focused unit and handshake tests were added. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): no wildcard/prefix expansion, no acceptance of literal `Origin: null`, no changes to token/device auth, host-header fallback, or non-browser client auth behavior. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [x] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #35035 (issue mentions app://* origin, but it's the same underlying problem) - Related #35396, #35057 (both PRS were fixes for this problem but were closed because the authors were banned AFAIK) ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` can now contain exact extension origins such as `chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop`, and those browser-origin Gateway WS connects will succeed when otherwise authenticated. - Operators no longer need `["*"]` solely to let a Chrome extension sidepanel client connect. - Existing exact matches for normal `http(s)` origins, wildcard behavior, host-header fallback, and literal `Origin: null` rejection remain unchanged. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` (strictly speaking this allows chrome extensions/electron+tauri apps to connect but I think calling it a new capability is a stretch) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `N/A` ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local Bun + Vitest - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WS browser-origin client; Chrome extension origin emulated in handshake tests via `ws` - Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.auth.mode="token"`, `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins=["chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop"]` ### Steps 1. Configure the Gateway with token auth and `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins=["chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop"]`. 2. Open a WS connection that sends `Origin: chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop` and a valid `connect` request. 3. Observe the handshake result. ### Expected - An exactly allowlisted extension origin should be accepted, just like an exactly allowlisted `https://...` origin. ### Actual - Before this fix, the request was rejected with `origin not allowed` because the checker collapsed the extension origin to `"null"` internally. - After this fix, the handshake succeeds and returns `hello-ok`. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: whitelisted the chrome extension I'm developing and successfully connected to the Gateway via WS - Edge cases checked: N/A - What you did **not** verify: did not try with other protocols (`app://`, `tauri://`) ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` - If yes, exact upgrade steps: `N/A` ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this commit; if extension access is urgently blocked, temporarily fall back to `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins=["*"]` until the regression is understood. - Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/origin-check.ts`; optionally restore prior `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` values in config. - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: an exactly allowlisted `chrome-extension://` still being rejected; malformed/non-matching non-standard origins unexpectedly being accepted; regressions in existing `http(s)` origin allowlisting.
